segments.text: Jesus and his disciples went to Jerusalem. As they were getting close, they came to the village of Bethphage on the Mount of Olives. Jesus sent two disciples on ahead,
telling them, {
Go into the village. Right there you'll find a donkey tied up with a colt. Untie them and bring them to me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone asks you what you're doing, just tell them, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and they will send them right away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This was to fulfill what the prophet said:
Tell the daughter of Zion, ‘Look, your King is coming to you. He is humble, and rides a donkey, and a colt, the offspring of a donkey.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The disciples went and did as Jesus had instructed.
They brought back the donkey and the colt. They placed their cloaks on them, and he sat on them.
Many people in the crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others cut branches from the trees and laid them on the road.
The crowds that went ahead of him and those that followed were all shouting, {
Hosanna to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!  Hosanna in the highest!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When Jesus arrived in Jerusalem, the whole city was in an uproar. {
Who is this?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they were asking.
This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee,
segments.speaker: The crowds

segments.text: the crowds replied.
Jesus went into the Temple, and threw out all the people buying and selling. He overturned the tables of the money-changers and the chairs of the dove-sellers.
He told them, {
Scripture says, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you've turned it into a den of thieves.
segments.speaker: The crowds

segments.text: The blind and the lame came to Jesus in the Temple, and he healed them.
But when the chief priests and religious teachers saw the wonderful miracles he did, and the children shouting in the Temple, {
Hosanna to the son of David,
segments.speaker: The crowds

segments.text: they were offended.
Do you hear what these children are saying?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked him.
Yes,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Haven't you ever read the Scripture that says, ‘You arranged for children and infants to give you perfect praise’?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus left them and went out of the city to stay at Bethany.
The next morning as he walked back into the city, he was hungry.
He saw a fig tree at the side of the road, so he went over to it but didn't find any fruit, only leaves. He told the fig tree, {
May you never ever produce fruit again!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately the fig tree withered.
The disciples were amazed to see this. {
How did the fig tree wither so suddenly?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
I tell you the truth,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,

If you really trust in God, and don't doubt him, you could not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even more. If you said to this mountain, ‘Get up and throw yourself into the sea,’ it would happen!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You will receive everything you ask for in prayer, as long as you trust in God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus went into the Temple. The chief priests and the ruling elders of the people came to him while he was teaching and asked, {
By what authority are you doing these things? Who gave you this authority?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I too will ask you a question,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
If you give me the answer, I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Where did the baptism of John come from? Was it from heaven, or was it from human beings?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They argued with each other.
If we say ‘It was from heaven,’ then he'll ask us why we didn't believe him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if we say, ‘It was from human beings,’ then the crowd will turn against us,  because they all consider John to be a prophet.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So they answered Jesus, {
We don't know.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I won't tell you by what authority I'm doing these things,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
But what do you think about this illustration? Once there was a man who had two sons. He went to the first son, and said, ‘Son, go and work in the vineyard today.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The son answered, ‘I won't,’ but afterwards he was sorry for what he said and he did go.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The man went to the second son, and told him the same thing. He replied, ‘I'll go,’ but he didn't.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of the two sons did what his father wanted?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The first,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
I tell you the truth: tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God before you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told them.

John came to show you the way to live right with God, and you didn't believe him, but the tax collectors and prostitutes did believe him. Later, when you saw what happened, you still didn't repent and believe him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Here's another illustration. There once was a man, a landowner, who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, made a winepress, and built a watchtower. He rented it to some tenant farmers, and then left to go to a different country.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At harvest-time, he sent his servants to the farmers to collect the fruit that belonged to him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the farmers attacked his servants. They beat one, killed another, and stoned another.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he sent more servants, but the farmers did the same things to them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So then he sent his son. ‘They will respect my son,’ he told himself.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the farmers, when they saw the son, said to each other, ‘Here's the heir! Come on! Let's kill him so we can take his inheritance!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the vineyard owner returns, what will he do to those farmers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The chief priests and elders said to Jesus, {
He will put to death those wicked men in the most awful way, and rent out the vineyard to other farmers who will be sure to give him his fruit at harvest-time.
segments.speaker: The chief priests and elders

segments.text: So haven't you read this in the Scriptures?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked them.
‘The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone. The Lord has done this, and it is wonderful in our eyes.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's why I'm telling you that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you. It will be given to a people who produce the right kind of fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Anyone who falls on this stone will be broken, but it will completely crush anyone it falls upon.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his illustrations, they realized he was speaking about them.
They wanted him arrested, but they were afraid of what the people would do because the people believed he was a prophet.
